content: Warren env vars for reaching burrow are warren-namespaced: WARREN_BURROW_SOCKET (default /var/run/burrow.sock), WARREN_BURROW_HOST + WARREN_BURROW_PORT for TCP, WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN for cross-container deploys where burrow runs with --auth. Namespacing means a sibling process running 'burrow' directly can keep its own BURROW_API_TOKEN / BURROW_COLOR without collision. Loaded once at startup via loadBurrowClientConfigFromEnv() in src/burrow-client/config.ts.

name: transport-error vs server-error mapping
description: Calls into HttpClient throw two flavors: structured BurrowError subclasses (rehydrated from burrow's {error: {code, message, hint}} envelope, see burrow lib/http-client.ts rehydrateError) AND raw fetch failures (TypeError: fetch failed with .cause.code = ENOENT/ECONNREFUSED/etc). The facade's withTransportMapping() and BurrowClient.probe() convert ONLY the second category into BurrowUnreachableError (a WarrenError subclass), letting BurrowError flow through untouched so callers can keep using instanceof NotFoundError / ValidationError as documented in burrow's lib. Detection walks the prototype chain because burrow subclasses override .name rather than .constructor.name. Warren's HTTP server should map BurrowUnreachableError to 503 and pass BurrowError envelopes through to the client.

name: HttpClient surface to wrap
description: @os-eco/burrow-cli exports HttpClient (from src/lib/http-client.ts) with five namespaces: burrows (up/list/get/tryGet/stop/resume/destroy), runs (create/get/tryGet/list/cancel/delete/stream), inbox (send/list/pending/cancel/count), events (tail/replay), agents (list/get/require/has). Plus top-level healthz() (auth-exempt). Construction is sync — Transport union is { kind: 'unix', path } | { kind: 'tcp', hostname, port }. For testing, fetch override is the boundary stub: HttpClientOptions.fetch = (input, init) => Promise<Response>. Don't reimplement these surfaces in warren — wrap and forward.
